111305.explanation: A referendum is a good tool for citizen participation.

111308.explanation: In principle, yes, but there may be reasons to disallow it.

111310.explanation: The horticulturist must take care of it themselves, but it does not necessarily have to be on their own property.

111311.explanation: It depends on the type of tree.

111321.explanation: That is already done as much as possible.

111323.explanation: Decline in members is not a reason for assistance. An energy crisis, for example, is.

111327.explanation: They must exist in the right places, regionally.

111328.explanation: This is a task for the national government.

111330.explanation: Necessary to promote flow. In the centers and preferably on the ground floor.

111332.explanation: A local party does not have authority over that.

111334.explanation: National approach, not as a single municipality while neighboring municipalities do not.

111336.explanation: Grows naturally, not ahead of time.

111337.explanation: Care is for and by everyone.

111342.explanation: But first densify and see who is where and put the right entrepreneurs in the right place. Old sites should be properly utilized first.

111344.explanation: It comes at the expense of our own residents, so do not do it.

111347.explanation: Not currently an issue, but if necessary, it should be possible.

111348.explanation: Traditions and neighborliness should be cherished.

111349.explanation: When someone wants to settle here, we will look into whether that can be done without nuisance.

111351.explanation: Four or five residential floors is fine.

111352.explanation: Housing is important, in whatever form.

111353.explanation: If a village center means a meeting place or community hall.

111354.explanation: That comes at the expense of greenery and housing.

111357.explanation: As long as the level of facilities is maintained.

111359.explanation: Regular maintenance of the waterways takes place.

111305.explanation: We believe that residents should be able to express themselves via a referendum on important issues. However, the topic must be suitable for this, and we see the referendum as a serious instrument that is only used for significant and exceptional decisions.

111308.explanation: The VVD stands for a proactive, cooperative government. We support entrepreneurship by farmers, but not without conditions. Initiatives such as shops or campsites are possible, provided they fit within the landscape, surroundings, and character of Brummen.

111310.explanation: This topic is of limited relevance in Brummen. In general, we agree that employers bear responsibility for housing seasonal workers. This prevents pressure on the housing market and ensures clear and decent facilities.

111311.explanation: The VVD stands for quality of nature, not just quantity. Not every tree adds the same value to the landscape. A mandatory one-to-one rule leads to unnecessary regulations and extra burdens for the government and residents, without guaranteeing better nature.

111314.explanation: The municipality does not need to organize a fireworks display itself. If a commercial party wants to organize an event around New Year's Eve, that should be possible. Besides, Brummen already has a local tradition, such as carbide shooting.

111318.explanation: Farmers must be given the opportunity to build a home on their own land. This must happen within clear rules and frameworks so that it fits the landscape and does not lead to fragmentation or a patchwork of buildings.

111321.explanation: The municipality is legally responsible for the maintenance and quality of school buildings. Investing in good and safe school buildings is part of our core tasks and is necessary for good education.

111323.explanation: If a sports club is in financial difficulty, extra municipal funding is not automatic. The primary responsibility lies with the club itself. The municipality already supports in other ways, including through the Het Sportkompas Foundation.

111325.explanation: The deployment of enforcement officers is currently insufficient to properly ensure safety and livability. We believe capacity should be increased. Additional BOAs (special enforcement officers) provide visibility, enforcement, and a safer feeling in the municipality.

111327.explanation: Shelter for victims of domestic violence is organized regionally with surrounding municipalities. This cooperation works well. Therefore, establishing a separate facility in Brummen is not necessary.

111328.explanation: The VVD supports the starter loan and has advocated for it in the past (2008). This instrument helps first-time buyers on the housing market and increases the chance that young people can continue living in Brummen.

111330.explanation: We want to build senior housing for our own elderly to promote the flow to smaller homes, but not more than that. The priority lies with owner-occupied homes for young families, so we better accommodate aging and keep Brummen vital.

111332.explanation: The VVD applauds if people with social assistance benefits voluntarily engage in social activities. However, we oppose a mandatory counter-performance that results in additional administration and bureaucracy; this unnecessarily costs money and manpower.

111334.explanation: The VVD wants to lower the income limit to the original 110%. Work must pay off, and poverty policy should not lead to a poverty trap in which people work less because they lose benefits.

111336.explanation: The number of charging points must grow with the use of electric cars. A good and balanced distribution throughout the villages is important so that charging options remain accessible to everyone.

111337.explanation: Everyone has the right to good care, but creating an additional enforceable right to care based on religion or culture does not align with liberal principles. In a sector with major staff shortages, quality and availability must be leading.

111341.explanation: The tourist tax has recently been raised twice. Holidays must remain affordable and the recreation sector is important for Brummen. Besides, VAT in this sector has already been increased. Another increase is undesirable.

111342.explanation: There is currently insufficient industrial terrain available in Brummen. The VVD wants more space for local entrepreneurs so that the economy becomes more robust. This must fit the scale and character of our municipality.

111344.explanation: We want to arrange this fairly through the housing ordinance. It cannot be that young people from Brummen cannot find housing for years, while others structurally are given priority for social rental homes.

111346.explanation: The electricity grid is already full and large-scale solar parks on agricultural land take up valuable space. We believe this does not fit in the landscape of Brummen and prefer other locations and solutions.

111347.explanation: School swimming is currently not a municipal task in Brummen and is not offered. Structurally allocating extra resources for this therefore does not fit within our current responsibilities.

111348.explanation: It is great if schools pay attention to regional language and culture, but this choice belongs to the schools themselves. Politics should not decide what is included in the educational program.

111349.explanation: The establishment of a coffee shop does not fit the village character of Brummen. We see no necessity and believe it does not contribute to livability and safety in a small municipality.

111351.explanation: We do not want high-rise buildings that damage the village character. Construction must fit Brummen. Therefore, we believe homes should not be built higher than four floors.

111352.explanation: The issue of room rental mainly occurs in large cities, not in Brummen. Extra regulations are therefore not needed here. Furthermore, banning room rental in general is not a good idea because it is an important housing form for, for example, students.

111353.explanation: Vibrancy in all neighborhoods is important, but not every neighborhood is big enough for a full-fledged village center. However, we see a meeting place or village hall as a realistic and valuable alternative for every neighborhood.

111354.explanation: More parking spaces are not necessarily needed. Existing parking spots should be used efficiently, for example by introducing blue zones so that parking stays available for visitors and entrepreneurs.

111357.explanation: We want to remain a recognizable and engaged municipality. But the reality is that it is increasingly difficult for a small municipality to independently carry out all tasks well and affordably. Cooperation or scaling up is needed to guarantee quality.

111358.explanation: A bypass can contribute to better accessibility and livability in Eerbeek. This requires a careful consideration of utility, necessity, and integration since available space is limited.

111359.explanation: Preventing water nuisance in Eerbeek is important. The municipality is already working on this. Further investments are needed to limit damage and nuisance in the future.

111305.explanation: GroenLinks supports the right to challenge, where residents and organizations can take over municipal topics. Making political considerations is not always suitable for a referendum. We stand for a strong democracy where the minority also has a voice.

111308.explanation: It is impossible to ALWAYS allow this. The municipality must think along with farmers in their search for a healthy business, both for the farming family and for nature.

111310.explanation: The housing shortage is too great to let the existing housing stock be 'filled' with labor migrants. Cheap labor brings extra costs that must be borne by the employers.

111311.explanation: Preferably two even. A green environment is very healthy for people and animals. Sometimes a tree must be cut down; then replanting a new tree is important. GroenLinks works on the replanting obligation; on the development of nature, so the municipality and residents must replant trees.

111314.explanation: In our municipality, carbide shooting is done with great pleasure on New Year's Eve. It is a social activity, which GroenLinks wants to continue. The surplus of illegal fireworks and the negative effects on humans and animals are too great.

111318.explanation: GroenLinks supports living on ancestral farms. Of course, a good plan must underlie this, and construction must be appropriate for the environment. GroenLinks has worked hard in recent years for village quality guides to be drawn up for this purpose.

111321.explanation: School buildings in Brummen are relatively new and of good quality. There will always be a need for money to adapt school buildings to current requirements. GroenLinks has helped ensure reserves have been built up for this in recent years.

111323.explanation: Sports clubs are not called clubs for nothing: they belong to the members. The municipality must provide good facilities for sports and exercise.

111325.explanation: Unfortunately, some neighborhoods in Brummen also have problems, and besides the police, BOAs are needed in terms of safety. GroenLinks has worked in recent years to develop better policies and allocate funds for better enforcement.

111327.explanation: Unfortunately, violence against women does not bypass our municipality. Our municipality must continually pay attention to this. If necessary, establishing a shelter for women fleeing domestic violence is certainly an option.

111328.explanation: GroenLinks wants to offer opportunities to our local youth. It is good that the municipality implements good housing policy on all fronts so that owner-occupied homes remain accessible even for older people with a smaller budget. For example, GroenLinks in Brummen has already voted for loans to make houses more sustainable.

111330.explanation: Many elderly people wish to live in "tailored" housing. The municipality must certainly continue building in this segment to get the housing market moving again. GroenLinks is working hard on establishing a Knarrenhof in Brummen.

111332.explanation: It is impossible to determine what is "socially meaningful." Moreover, not everyone is simply able to fulfill this. We definitely support policies where people gain added value from social involvement.

111334.explanation: The cost of living is rising rapidly; we see many people getting into financial trouble, including those who are employed. GroenLinks wants to help these families quickly so the problems don’t get worse. Otherwise, it will cost the municipality even more money to resolve later.

111336.explanation: Home and street charging must become easier. For GroenLinks, electric driving is the norm, and the municipality must ensure that residents have the possibility to charge their electric cars in their own neighborhood.

111337.explanation: Care must be accessible to everyone. It is not always possible to organize care completely tailored to individuals. However, the municipality must ensure diversity in care offerings through its procurement policy.

111341.explanation: Because the municipality receives less money from the national government and wants to maintain facilities, visitors to our beautiful municipality will also have to contribute to the costs; therefore, sometimes an increase in tourist tax is necessary.

111342.explanation: GroenLinks prefers to allocate the limited space to housing and nature. Maintaining the local economy is important. The development of a sustainable and green economy is important, but right now we mainly need housing and preserving the open and green character.

111344.explanation: By opening an asylum seekers’ center (AZC) in Brummen, the municipality can assign fewer regular homes to status holders through regional distribution. That means neighboring municipalities must house more status holders, and more homes in Brummen remain available to other residents.

111346.explanation: The energy transition is an important issue, and we need all sustainable sources. For the next 30 years, GroenLinks foresees that it will be unavoidable to use agricultural land for the energy transition.

111347.explanation: Growing up healthy and safely is important, and affordable swimming lessons are part of that for GroenLinks. Swimming lessons have become a luxury, causing many children to grow up unable to swim.

111348.explanation: The substantive offering in education is not a municipal responsibility.

111349.explanation: We see places in our municipality where nuisance arises due to drug dealing. One way to combat this is by regulating drug sales.

111351.explanation: GroenLinks opposes a Manhattan-like development on the IJssel River, but to realize enough housing, building upwards is an option. GroenLinks has worked hard in this regard for apartments at Van Limburg Stirumplein (Brummen, 5 floors) and the Burgersterrein (Eerbeek, 4 floors).

111352.explanation: There is also a need for student housing in Brummen, which should actually be encouraged because it can help solve part of the housing shortage. However, the rooms must be well managed; GroenLinks intends to make agreements with owners about this.

111353.explanation: Neighborhood centers are important supporters of pleasant coexistence: places where people can meet, associations find shelter, and facilities are offered. Our policy is aligned with this. Whether a village can sustain its own village center depends on its size.

111354.explanation: GroenLinks focuses on a healthy society where cyclists and pedestrians have priority over all other traffic. Parking spaces will be created according to the municipal standard, and that is sufficient.

111357.explanation: The municipality must gain more capacity. GroenLinks wants to maintain service levels for residents, and for that, a merger with another municipality (or multiple) is necessary.

111358.explanation: We see increasing traffic pressure in Eerbeek, especially in the center. GroenLinks finds it important to ensure better traffic flow for (heavy) traffic in Eerbeek with the future in mind.

111359.explanation: There is enough money in the budget to properly carry out the municipality's own task in water management (keeping watercourses open). The municipality must collaborate with the water board, province, and industry to achieve a good balance in water management.

111305.explanation: As residents, we govern our own municipality. When a topic is highly debated, a consultative referendum can be a fair and transparent way to make a decision.

111308.explanation: Permission to open a store or campsite depends on location, type of business, and size of activities.

111310.explanation: Employers share responsibility for good housing. It does not have to be on their own premises. A condition is that the employment contract must be separate from the rental contract.

111311.explanation: Greenery is urgently needed for cooling, clean air, and a pleasant neighborhood.

111314.explanation: In principle no, but if residents do not experience nuisance and public safety is guaranteed, alternatives can be considered.

111318.explanation: It could only be allowed under stringent conditions.

111321.explanation: No yes, no no. If necessary, the municipality will not shy away from its responsibility.

111323.explanation: Sports strengthen health, meeting one another, and community spirit. And everyone should be able to participate, regardless of income.

111325.explanation: It is better to invest in prevention to increase the citizens' own responsibility.

111327.explanation: A regional shelter is necessary to provide a safe place for people who are not safe at home.

111328.explanation: The municipality must commit to building affordable homes.

111330.explanation: The housing market is completely stuck, partly because elderly people cannot move to smaller homes. That is why we want to build that type of home as soon as possible. This will also free up houses for other housing seekers.

111332.explanation: People are not on welfare because they don’t want to work. They need a stable foundation, affordable housing, and trust from the municipality. Extra demands and conditions only create more uncertainty and cause new problems.

111334.explanation: Having a good and fair social minimum policy in the municipality is necessary. Note: the statement is confusing.

111337.explanation: Both the care provider and the care recipient must respect every religion, culture, or belief.

111342.explanation: In the municipality of Brummen, we focus on preserving the current industrial areas.

111344.explanation: There are multiple target groups eligible for priority. Priority depends on the individual circumstances of the person seeking housing.

111346.explanation: Under conditions such as a maximum period, good landscape integration, and restoring the land to its original state.

111347.explanation: It is important that all children receive swimming lessons. Parents with low incomes can be financially supported through minimum income policies.

111348.explanation: It is important that students learn Dutch well at school and can speak and write it properly.

111349.explanation: The municipality monitors a safe and healthy lifestyle and living environment.

111351.explanation: High-rise apartment buildings do not fit in Brummen's village landscape.

111352.explanation: We do not need to ban it. It can be allowed under certain conditions.

111353.explanation: For the municipality of Brummen, we want to realize a multifunctional center in the Brummen core and a 'Pakhuus' in the Eerbeek core.

111357.explanation: The PvdA will only support it if there is better service while maintaining all facilities.

111358.explanation: A ring road around Eerbeek is desirable, but solely for Eerbeek it is not a high priority. For Loenen, however, it is.

111359.explanation: The sewer system must comply with national standards, taking into account the requirements posed by climate adaptation.

111305.explanation: D66 wants to maintain ongoing dialogue with residents, not just occasional conversations. A referendum is an expensive tool and only involves residents briefly. Moreover, many issues are too complex to answer with a simple 'for' or 'against.'

111308.explanation: Tourism and recreation keep the municipality of Brummen lively. Farm shops, ice cream parlors, or B&Bs strengthen our local economy. That is why we embrace such initiatives by farmers, with clear agreements regarding traffic, nature, and neighbors.

111310.explanation: Seasonal workers are needed, but their housing must be properly arranged. D66 believes employers should take responsibility and arrange decent, safe housing on their own premises, with clear rules. This prevents nuisance and protects workers.

111311.explanation: Trees make the municipality of Brummen greener, cooler, and healthier. Cutting down trees must mean replanting, so we don't lose greenery bit by bit. Where necessary, this can be with multiple young trees or better locations, so the greenery truly returns and becomes stronger.

111314.explanation: With a nationwide fireworks ban in sight, social gatherings around New Year's are extra important. D66 therefore wants to continue supporting carbide shooting as a meeting moment on New Year's Eve.

111318.explanation: Housing is urgent, also for (farming) families. But new houses in the countryside mean sealing land and pressure on the landscape and nature. D66 does choose for residential use on farms, splitting and shared housing on existing farms, and primarily building in the village centers.

111321.explanation: Good schools are the foundation for equal opportunities. D66 Brummen wants to keep investing in school buildings: much has already been renewed and a multi-year maintenance plan has been established. This ensures all children have a safe, healthy place to learn.

111323.explanation: We want strong sports clubs, but structurally supporting them with municipal money is not always fair or sustainable. D66 chooses assistance that helps: advice, sustainability improvements, use of facilities, and support during temporary emergencies. This keeps clubs healthy and the costs fair.

111325.explanation: Safety starts with visibility on the street. D66 wants more enforcement officers (BOAs), so issues like nuisance and unsafe traffic can be addressed faster and there is more preventive contact with young people. This relieves the police and keeps living pleasant.

111327.explanation: No one should feel unsafe at home. D66 Brummen wants a low-threshold, safe shelter for those fleeing domestic violence, together with regional partners. Quick help, discreet, with guidance towards a new start.

111328.explanation: It must also be possible for first-time buyers to purchase a home. However, starter loans only drive up prices. It's better to provide more suitable homes and give priority in new housing projects to buyers who live or work in the municipality.

111330.explanation: Many elderly people want to downsize but remain in their own environment. D66 wants more affordable and accessible housing. This gives comfort to the elderly and frees up family homes for starters and families. A win-win for housing flow.

111332.explanation: We should primarily ensure that people can participate and find paid work. That requires tailored solutions.

111334.explanation: Many people just fall outside support schemes, we understand that. Yet funds for poverty policy are limited. D66 chooses targeted support with customized help and fast debt assistance, rather than stretching limits.

111336.explanation: Electric driving is growing, but charging points should not increase parking pressure in villages. D66 opposes simply allocating more parking spaces for charging. We opt for smart distribution, charging where it fits, and especially more charging points without losing parking spaces indiscriminately.

111337.explanation: Let's not pretend that someone with a different faith, culture or conviction cannot provide good care. The discussion should not be about who provides it, but about what: expert, respectful care for everyone.

111341.explanation: Tourism brings liveliness and income, but also costs the municipality investments in facilities and infrastructure. D66 opts for a fair fee and smart investments in recreation, nature, and amenities from which everyone benefits.

111342.explanation: Business growth can strengthen jobs and facilities, so expansion sounds logical. D66 is in favor but with care: first utilize and make existing sites more sustainable, then targeted expansion in the right place with good accessibility and landscape integration.

111344.explanation: Waiting for social housing is frustrating; giving priority feels unfair. Still, the municipality of Brummen has the legal task to house status holders, so people can move on from shelters. D66 therefore supports targeted priority and extra building.

111346.explanation: In Brummen, we have established rules that solar panels on agricultural land must meet. These rules should be evaluated and adjusted where necessary, but in principle, it must be possible to place solar panels on agricultural land.

111347.explanation: Not every child gets the chance to learn swimming at home. With school swimming, the municipality of Brummen increases safety and equal opportunities, especially in a water-rich municipality. D66 therefore wants municipal support, with attention to costs and efficient organization.

111348.explanation: It is important to be mindful of local culture and customs. Regional dialects are part of that.

111349.explanation: Concerned about nuisance? D66 chooses for a regulated coffee shop: clear rules, locations away from schools, supervision, and enforcement. This removes trade from the streets, makes usage more manageable, and increases safety.

111351.explanation: More homes are urgently needed; building upwards is then very logical. But 'high-rise residential flats' sound enormously unattractive. D66 does choose multi-storey apartments in suitable locations, with a good, green appearance.

111352.explanation: Shared housing can relieve the tight market; at the same time, there are concerns about nuisance. D66 supports rental with clear rules: quality requirements, a maximum per home, good registration, and enforcement. This helps starters and students without burdening neighborhoods.

111353.explanation: Meeting in one's own village keeps clubs and communities strong. D66 wants an accessible, multifunctional center where needed and where it fits—preferably by smart use/expansion of existing buildings.

111354.explanation: D66 advocates for livable village centers with greenery, safe walking and cycling routes, and pleasant public spaces. Reserving space for extra parking must be done smartly so it does not detract from these facilities.

111357.explanation: In 2025 there was a unanimous decision to investigate merging the municipality with another municipality. This followed a comprehensive study where all possible options were considered. Remaining independent will jeopardize the quality of services to residents.

111358.explanation: Good and safe traffic flow is important. D66 Brummen does not see a ring road as realistic given the location next to the Veluwe.

111359.explanation: Attention must be given to water management in public spaces. On private land, owners are responsible themselves.

111305.explanation: The CDA finds participation of the municipality's residents extremely important. A referendum reduces complex decisions to a yes or no choice and does not do justice to the complexity of many proposals.

111308.explanation: We advocate for a vital countryside with space for recreation, nature, and the agricultural sector. The CDA wants a flexible attitude from the municipality, with opportunities to reward interesting initiatives. It always depends on the initiative!

111310.explanation: For short-term laborers, housing on company premises, vacant buildings/homes, and pensions and hotels designated for this purpose are suitable, provided good living conditions, safety, registration, and management are guaranteed.

111311.explanation: Not every tree removed in the municipality needs to be compensated for on site. However, it is important that tree felling does not happen arbitrarily and that deforestation is prevented. In general, we greatly value our green residential and work environment!

111314.explanation: To offer an alternative to privately set off fireworks, the CDA supports a fireworks show, provided it can be organized safely and possibly in cooperation with neighboring municipalities. Traditional card bidding shooting can continue in a well-organized manner.

111318.explanation: Where it concerns the schemes 'Red for Red', second business home or house splitting, for example for informal care of children for parents on farms, and there are no insurmountable adverse consequences for the landscape.

111321.explanation: The school buildings in Brummen are generally of good quality. Initially, the school boards are responsible for maintenance, as they receive funding for this from the Ministry of Education. The municipality is responsible when it comes to new construction.

111323.explanation: Associations form the backbone of a living community. Support can of course never be unconditional. In principle, sports clubs should be financially self-sufficient. Support can be requested from the Sportkompas foundation.

111325.explanation: CDA believes that a stronger sense of safety does not directly correlate with an increase in the number of BOAs (special enforcement officers). Neighborhood solidarity and 'looking out for each other' do achieve this. CDA would like to make this the norm again (where it is not yet the case).

111327.explanation: If there is a need for it, that is certainly desirable.

111328.explanation: As part of obtaining a mortgage. Initially, financial institutions were naturally created for this purpose.

111330.explanation: The population of Brummen is also aging, and the housing program of Brummen must be adjusted accordingly. This also promotes mobility within the municipality.

111332.explanation: It is important for people with welfare benefits to continue participating in society. The scale at which this happens should take place in good consultation with the Team for Each Other. Every resident matters and counts!

111334.explanation: The income limit must rise in line with inflation.

111336.explanation: As the share of electric cars in the vehicle fleet increases, more charging points will also be needed.

111337.explanation: Personal connection with the caregiver at the most vulnerable moments in a person’s life is important. Freedom of choice is a right, but there is no right to compensation for it. Unfortunately, this will come under further pressure in the future due to a shortage of care personnel.

111341.explanation: Now that the VAT on overnight stays has been increased nationwide in 2026, CDA finds it inappropriate to further raise the tourist tax. Tourism should not become a cash cow for the municipality.

111342.explanation: New business parks in the municipality of Brummen will mainly affect the relocation of companies from a nearby region. Brummen is better off focusing on renewal and improvement of the current area and cooperation on regional sites.

111344.explanation: The municipality must contribute to housing refugees with a residence permit, just as it does for other highly urgent housing seekers.

111346.explanation: CDA is cautious about allowing large-scale solar parks on agricultural land.

111347.explanation: The municipality currently provides no funding for school swimming. We consider learning to swim and paying for it a primary responsibility of parents/guardians, not schools. Parents can already turn to the municipality if they have financing problems.

111348.explanation: CDA supports regional languages, local dialects, and dialects, albeit to a limited extent and on a voluntary basis.

111349.explanation: Given the scale of the municipality of Brummen and its enforcement capabilities, no coffeeshop fits within the municipal boundaries.

111351.explanation: There is no place for real high-rise buildings in the centers of the municipality. High-rises do not sufficiently align with the current village character.

111352.explanation: Renting and letting individual rooms can be a (temporary) solution to the housing problem for part of the population.

111353.explanation: All villages already have more or less their own village center, and this can be further strengthened to increase their own identity and community spirit.

111354.explanation: Parking does not pose a major problem in our village centers.

111357.explanation: CDA Brummen favors municipal redivision or extensive cooperation to solve existing vulnerabilities, to strengthen administrative capacity, and to have a greater strategic influence on regional developments as a larger municipality.

111358.explanation: There must be continued attention for improving the handling of traffic flows (freight traffic) at places in Eerbeek where this currently leads to dangerous situations.

111359.explanation: Groundwater nuisance is a serious problem in some parts of Eerbeek. Together with the province of Gelderland and the Water Board, the municipality must seek solutions.

statements.title: Brummen should remain an independent municipality.
Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: For every tree cut down in the municipality, a new one must be planted.
More small rental and owner-occupied homes for elderly people should be built in Brummen.
The municipality should invest money in improving all school buildings during the next council term.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet in de komende

titleParts.glossary: The municipal council is elected for a period of four years.

titleParts.text: geld steken in het verbeteren van alle schoolgebouwen.

statements.title: Brummen should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

statements.title: More business parks (industrial sites) should be developed in Brummen.
It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
A women's shelter (a refuge for victims of domestic violence) should be established in Brummen.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: The municipality must prohibit landlords from renting out separate rooms instead of whole houses.
The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
There should be a ring road around Eerbeek.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The residents of Brummen should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Brummen moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: More high-rise residential buildings should be allowed in Brummen.
The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: The municipality must make it possible for all primary schools to include Low Saxon.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality must allow solar panels on agricultural land.
All villages in the municipality must have their own village center.
The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
The municipality must continue to make funds available for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar blijven stellen  voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: First-time homebuyers should be able to receive a starter loan from the municipality.

titleParts.text: Mensen die voor het eerst een huis willen kopen, moeten van de gemeente een

titleParts.glossary: An extra loan on top of your mortgage, which you can only get when buying a house for the first time.

statements.title: The municipality should allow the establishment of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: There should be more parking spaces in the town centers.

titleParts.text: Er moeten meer parkeerplaatsen komen in de

titleParts.glossary: A village or small town that is part of the municipality.

statements.title: The municipality of Brummen must invest more money in preventing floods in Eerbeek.
